summary: GEMORA MVP end-to-end review. Backend: 25/27 pytest passing (auth, seed idempotency, catalog, vernacular search, cart double-sell 409, checkout mock-pay, dispatch→activation→vault, revoke, RBAC, Emergent session bearer, consultation all pass). Frontend: home / shop (8 cards) / product detail / verify page render cleanly with palette + testids. The 2 failing tests expose ONE CRITICAL backend bug (ephemeral Ed25519 key regenerated on every backend restart → all previously-issued certificates return SUSPICIOUS forever, breaking the crown-jewel verify feature).

critical.issue: CRITICAL — Ed25519 private key is ephemeral. backend/.env has ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX="" so server.py:54 generates a NEW random key on every backend startup. Every certificate signed with the previous key can never re-verify against the new public key, so /api/verify/... returns status=SUSPICIOUS instead of AUTHENTIC even for legitimate seeded/dispatched units. Reproduced twice: currently mounted certs have public_key_hex=0002a5… while ED25519_PUBLIC_HEX=1b2821…. Also seen the key rotate between two curl calls minutes apart (any backend reload silently breaks all prior certs). Fix: persist ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX in backend/.env (32 raw bytes, hex-encoded) OR generate once and write to .env on first boot. Until fixed, GEMORA's core trust guarantee is unusable.

minor.issue: Anonymous first paint logs 401s in browser console (auth-probe pattern). Consider treating a 401 on these endpoints as `not signed in` and suppressing the network error in the client, or add a lightweight /api/auth/whoami that returns 200 with null user for cleaner logs.
Model uses `concern` (not `topic`). Endpoint returns booking dict directly (not `{booking: {...}}`) — docstring/tests should match. Not a bug; noted for API doc consistency.

integration_issues.flow: Verify page for a legitimate activated cert
integration_issues.issue: Always shows SUSPICIOUS due to backend ephemeral-key bug (see backend/critical/1). Frontend rendering itself is correct; it faithfully displays whatever backend returns.

action_items: URGENT: Persist Ed25519 private key. In backend/.env set ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX to a stable 32-byte hex value (e.g., `python -c "from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519 import Ed25519PrivateKey; from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import serialization as s; k=Ed25519PrivateKey.generate(); print(k.private_bytes(s.Encoding.Raw,s.PrivateFormat.Raw,s.NoEncryption()).hex())"`), then restart backend and re-seed. After that /api/verify/:qr_token will return AUTHENTIC for freshly-signed certs and stay stable across restarts.
Optional: on startup, if ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX is empty, generate once and PERSIST to .env instead of only in-memory — prevents this footgun.
Optional: silence anonymous 401 console noise on /auth/me + /cart during first paint.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:50-55 — ephemeral key generation without persistence is unsafe for a product whose entire value proposition is verifiable authenticity. Refuse to boot without ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX, or write a generated one to disk atomically.
server.py is 1096 lines — consider splitting into routers/{auth,catalog,verify,cart,orders,admin,consultation}.py plus core/{crypto,security,db,seed}.py for maintainability.

server.py:743 raises HTTPException from _mark_paid after some reservations already flipped to sold → partial commit state. Mongo without txns can't be truly atomic here; consider wrapping in a session/transaction if replica set is available, or add a compensating rollback loop.
server.py:615 sets `httponly=False` on the gemora_anon cookie (fine for readable anon key) but path scope + samesite=none+secure=true is correct. Good.

success_rate.frontend: 100% render, all pages load, 8 product cards on /shop, verify page renders correct card

test_credentials: admin@gemora.in / admin@1234, priya@example.com / priya@1234 — both work; sourced from /app/memory/test_credentials.md
seed_data_creation: Idempotent POST /api/dev/seed produces 8 products, 2 units per serialised product, 1 activated Ed25519-signed cert per serialised product. During testing I purged & re-seeded certificates+units+carts+reservations+orders once to validate the verify chain within a single key epoch (it worked). Buyer verified_items may need a re-seed if further UI E2E is required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent sets ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX in backend/.env and restarts + POST /api/dev/seed, run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_gemora_backend.py -v`. All 27 tests should pass. Pytest is configured with -n 2 --dist loadscope (see /app/backend/pytest.ini) — the single TestGemora class keeps sequential state on one worker. If seed already has old certs with previous public key, purge db.certificates + db.units + db.reservations + db.carts + db.orders + db.verified_items before re-seeding.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE — server.py loads ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX from env at startup (lines 50-55). backend/.env contains ED25519_PRIVATE_KEY_HEX="" (empty). So the `if _priv_hex` branch is False and Ed25519PrivateKey.generate() creates a random key that only lives for the process lifetime. On every supervisor restart / code hot-reload, the key rotates. Certificates persisted in Mongo carry the old public_key_hex + a signature made with the old private key; the new process cannot verify them → verify() returns AUTHENTIC only if signature verification succeeds, else SUSPICIOUS. REPRO: (1) POST /api/dev/seed, (2) note public_key from GET /api/, (3) sudo supervisorctl restart backend, (4) GET /api/ shows a NEW public_key, (5) GET /api/verify/<any_seed_qr_token> returns SUSPICIOUS. MITIGATION: generate a fixed key and put its hex in backend/.env; supervisorctl restart backend; POST /api/dev/seed (idempotent, so purge certs+units first if you want fresh signed data).
